article_title: Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submissions — July 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: HUB Cycling protected bike lane on Winston Street
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a proposal from HUB Cycling to build a protected bike lane on Winston Street. A protected bike lane is a lane separated from car traffic by a physical barrier, like a curb or posts. This is not yet approved — Council is set to vote on the idea at an upcoming meeting. If approved, the new lane could change traffic patterns and parking along Winston Street, which may affect nearby businesses.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date and attend or submit comments to have your say on how this could affect your business.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 1.1 HUB Cycling - Protected Bike Lane on Winston Street
agenda_briefs.article_title: Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submissions Agenda — July 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Council to vote on lease security for Hart House Restaurant operator
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposal regarding the long-term lease security of Hart House Restaurant, which is operated by Jan Evanski under the name Mintara. The decision would affect the terms of the lease and the operator's ability to stay in the space. This is a proposed change that has not yet been approved. Business owners should watch for the council's decision, as it could set a precedent for how the city handles long-term leases for commercial tenants in city-owned properties.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect lease terms for city-owned property tenants.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 1.2 Jan Evanski - Mintara Operations and Long-Term Lease Security of Hart House Restaurant
agenda_briefs.article_title: Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submissions Agenda — July 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby e-scooter safety rules and enforcement
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a proposed bylaw to regulate e-scooter safety and usage in Burnaby. The proposal would set new rules for where and how e-scooters can be ridden, and how the rules will be enforced. This is not yet law — Council is set to vote on the proposal at an upcoming meeting. If approved, the new rules could affect businesses that offer e-scooter rentals or have delivery services that use e-scooters.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision date — if you operate an e-scooter rental or delivery business, this could affect your operations.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 1.3 Martin Kendell - E-Scooter Safety Usage
agenda_briefs.article_title: Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submissions Agenda — July 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Speed bumps on Elwell Street near Edmonds Park
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposal to install speed bumps on Elwell Street, the section that runs next to Edmonds Park. The goal is to slow down traffic and make the street safer for people walking, biking, and playing in the park. This is a proposed change — it has not been approved yet. If passed, the speed bumps would be installed on the street itself, not on the park side.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Contact your Burnaby City Councillor before the vote to share your support or concerns about the proposed speed bumps.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 1.4 Michelle Danisz - Implementation of Speedbumps along Elwell St. Adjacent to Edmonds Park
agenda_briefs.article_title: Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submissions Agenda — July 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Vacant property rules for Royal Oak Avenue and Grimmer Street
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a proposal from Shawn Shahrokhi regarding a vacant property at Royal Oak Avenue and Grimmer Street. This is an agenda item for an upcoming Burnaby Council meeting — it has not been approved yet. The proposal may involve new rules or changes for how vacant land is used or developed in that area. Business owners should watch for the council decision, as it could affect property development or land use in Burnaby.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date and attend or submit comments to have your say on this proposed vacant property rule.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 1.5 Shawn Shahrokhi - Vacant Property at Royal Oak Avenue and Grimmer Street
agenda_briefs.article_title: Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submissions Agenda — July 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Defer development fees on coach house permits in Burnaby
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a request from Smallworks Ltd. to defer payment of Area-Specific Development Cost Charges (ACC) and Development Cost Charges (DCC) on coach house building permits. These are fees developers normally pay to the city to help fund infrastructure like roads, sewers, and parks. If approved, the deferral would allow the builder to pay these charges later, which could lower upfront costs for new coach house projects. The decision has not been made yet — Council is set to vote on this proposal at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, this could affect the cost and timing of your coach house project.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 1.6 Smallworks Ltd. - Request to Defer ACC/DCC on Coach House Permits
agenda_briefs.article_title: Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submissions Agenda — July 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Temporary shelter and warming centre at 7320 Buller Avenue
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposal to open a temporary shelter and emergency warming centre at 7320 Buller Avenue. If approved, this facility would provide overnight shelter and a place to warm up for people experiencing homelessness. The proposed bylaw would allow the city to use this property for shelter services on a temporary basis. Business owners in the area should be aware that this could change the neighbourhood's character and may affect foot traffic, parking, and local business operations.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect your business operations and neighbourhood conditions.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 1.7 Temporary Shelter and Emergency Warming Centre at 7320 Buller Avenue
agenda_briefs.article_title: Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submissions Agenda — July 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby council to consider 2026 property tax spike and reform
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a proposal from a resident, Yannick Cudennec, calling for a major property tax increase in 2026 and a shift to a more progressive tax structure. A 'progressive' tax structure means higher-value properties would be taxed at a higher rate than lower-value ones. This is not a final decision — council is set to vote on whether to study or adopt this idea at an upcoming meeting. If approved, it could significantly raise costs for owners of high-value commercial and residential properties in Burnaby starting in 2026.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date and attend or submit comments to have your say on this proposed tax change.
agenda_briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail, Professional Services
agenda_briefs.source_body: 1.8 Yannick Cudennec - 2026 Property Tax Spike and Call for Progressive Structural Reform
agenda_briefs.article_title: Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submissions Agenda — July 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to vote on Edmonds Community School playground grant
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council will consider a request from Edmonds Community School for a playground grant. This is a proposed decision — Council has not yet voted on it. If approved, the grant would help fund a new playground at the school. The item is on the agenda for an upcoming Council meeting, where Council members will debate and decide whether to approve the funding.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 2.1.1 Edmonds Community School - Request for Playground Grant
agenda_briefs.article_title: Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submissions Agenda — July 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to vote on Rowing BC sport hosting grant
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a request from Rowing BC for an off-cycle Sport Hosting Grant. This is a proposed decision — the grant has not yet been approved. If approved, the grant would help Rowing BC bring a rowing event to Burnaby, which could mean more visitors and business for local hotels, restaurants, and retail shops. The decision is up for a vote at an upcoming council meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, prepare for increased customer traffic during the event.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 2.1.2 Rowing BC - Request for Off-Cycle Sport Hosting Grant
agenda_briefs.article_title: Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submissions Agenda — July 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Union Street traffic calming measures in Burnaby
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a request from a resident, Christian Chiappetta, to add traffic calming measures on Union Street. This is a proposed change — it has not been approved yet. If passed, the measures could include speed bumps, narrowed lanes, or other changes to slow down traffic. The goal is to make the street safer for pedestrians and cyclists. Business owners on or near Union Street should be aware that these changes could affect vehicle access, parking, and delivery routes.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Contact Burnaby City Hall or attend the council meeting to share how traffic changes on Union Street would affect your business.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 2.2.1 Christian Chiappetta - Request for Union Street Traffic Calming Measures
agenda_briefs.article_title: Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submissions Agenda — July 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby bylaw changes for bike lanes and traffic rules
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on amendments to Burnaby's Street and Traffic Bylaw 1961. The changes, requested by HUB Cycling, would update rules for bike lanes, cycling infrastructure, and related traffic signs. If approved, the bylaw would affect how streets are used by cyclists and drivers. The proposal is on the council agenda for discussion and a vote — it has not been passed yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if passed, new bike lane rules could affect your business's street access or parking.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 2.2.2 HUB Cycling - Amendments to the Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw 1961
agenda_briefs.article_title: Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submissions Agenda — July 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to vote on Michael J. Fox Theatre operating grant
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed operating grant for the Michael J. Fox Theatre, submitted by the Burnaby Board of Education. If approved, this grant would provide funding to help cover the theatre's day-to-day operating costs. The decision has not yet been made — Council will consider the proposal at an upcoming meeting. Business owners who use the theatre for events or rely on its programming should watch for the outcome, as a grant decision could affect the venue's availability or rental fees.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, the grant could affect theatre rental rates or availability for your events.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 3.1 Burnaby Board of Education - Michael J. Fox Theatre Operating Grant
agenda_briefs.article_title: Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submissions Agenda — July 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Metro Vancouver Board to consider regional planning updates
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a report from the Metro Vancouver Board meeting of May 29, 2026. This report outlines proposed updates to regional growth strategy and infrastructure plans. If approved, these changes could affect how land is used and how services like water and waste are managed across the region. Business owners should be aware that new rules or fees may follow, but nothing has been decided yet.
agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Professional Services
agenda_briefs.source_body: 3.2 Metro Vancouver - Board in Brief - May 29, 2026
agenda_briefs.article_title: Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submissions Agenda — July 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Metro 2050 land-use amendment for Surrey (105–175A Street)
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a proposed amendment to the Metro 2050 regional growth strategy, known as a Type 3 amendment. This is a bylaw (No. 1463, 2026) from the City of Surrey that would change land-use designations for properties along 105 to 175A Street. If approved, it could affect future development, density, and infrastructure planning in that area. The decision is not final — it is up for discussion and a vote at a future council meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision date — this amendment could affect development potential on your property.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 3.3 Metro Vancouver - Metro 2050 Type 3 Proposed Amendment, Bylaw No. 1463, 2026 City of Surrey (105 – 175A Street)
agenda_briefs.article_title: Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submissions Agenda — July 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Independent review of the Fraser River Tunnel Project
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a request from the City of Delta for an independent review of the Fraser River Tunnel Project. This proposed review would examine the project's impacts on traffic, the environment, and local communities before any final decisions are made. The outcome could affect transportation and business access in the region.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Monitor council's decision on this review — it could affect future transportation routes and business logistics in the area.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 3.4 City of Delta - Independent Review of the Fraser River Tunnel Project
agenda_briefs.article_title: Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submissions Agenda — July 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Oak Bay seeks Burnaby funding for climate action program
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a request from the District of Oak Bay for funding under the Local Government Climate Action Program. This program provides money to local governments for projects that reduce greenhouse gas emissions and help communities adapt to climate change. The decision is on the agenda for an upcoming meeting and has not yet been approved. If approved, the funding would support Oak Bay's climate initiatives, but it does not directly create new rules or costs for Burnaby businesses at this time.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 3.5 District of Oak Bay - Local Government Climate Action Program Funding
agenda_briefs.article_title: Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submissions Agenda — July 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to consider BC Nurses' Union award
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a proposal to support the BC Nurses' Union Health Care Champion Award. This is an annual award that recognizes individuals or groups who have made outstanding contributions to health care in British Columbia. The decision is on the agenda for an upcoming meeting and has not yet been approved. If passed, it would show the City of Burnaby's support for recognizing health care champions.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 3.6 BC Nurses' Union - Annual BC Nurses’ Union Health Care Champion Award
agenda_briefs.article_title: Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submissions Agenda — July 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby council to vote on cannabis smoke rules in public spaces
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposed action to address cannabis smoke in public spaces and residential neighborhoods. If approved, this could mean new rules or restrictions on where people can smoke cannabis outdoors, including parks, sidewalks, and near homes. The proposal specifically targets smoking in private yards near shared property lines, and calls for stronger enforcement at Brentwood, Station Square, and Metrotown malls, transit stations, and parks. The proposal is still under consideration — it has not been passed yet. Business owners, especially those in the cannabis and hospitality sectors, should watch for the council decision as it may affect where customers can legally consume cannabis near their businesses.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect where customers can consume cannabis near your business, including potential new restrictions on smoking in private yards near shared property lines.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 3.7 G - Request for Action on Cannabis Smoke in Burnaby's Public Spaces and Residential Neighborhoods

attachments.title: G - Request for Action on Cannabis Smoke in Burnaby's Public Spaces and Resident

agenda_briefs.article_title: Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submissions Agenda — July 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby water meter project safety concerns raised
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a submission from resident Lynne-Marie-Paule citing fire and health risks from lithium batteries in the City of Burnaby's water meter project. The submission details risks of electrical shock, chemical exposure, thermal runaway, and toxic gas emissions (e.g., hydrogen fluoride) from damaged or defective batteries, referencing multiple fire incidents involving lithium-ion batteries. No changes have been approved yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on this safety concern — it could affect how the water meter project is carried out on your property.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 3.8 Lynne-Marie-Paule - Safety Concerns Regarding City of Burnaby Water Meter Project

attachments.title: Lynne-Marie-Paule - Safety Concerns Regarding City of Burnaby Water Meter Projec

agenda_briefs.article_title: Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submissions Agenda — July 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to review Stoney Creek Watershed plan
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a review of the Stoney Creek Watershed, as recommended by the Stoney Creek Environment Committee. This is a proposed review — it has not been approved yet. The review could lead to new rules or guidelines for development, construction, and land use near the creek. Business owners in the area should watch for updates, as this could affect future building or renovation projects.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this review could affect future development or construction near Stoney Creek.
agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Natural Resources
agenda_briefs.source_body: 3.9 Stoney Creek Environment Committee - Stoney Creek Watershed Review
agenda_briefs.article_title: Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submissions Agenda — July 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Federal regulations for aircraft noise in Burnaby
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a request from Stuart Ramsey to push for new federal rules on aircraft noise. This is a proposal, not a done deal. If approved, Burnaby would ask the federal government to create stricter noise limits for planes flying over the city. The goal is to reduce noise for residents and businesses near flight paths. The decision is up for debate at an upcoming council meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, it could lead to federal changes affecting noise levels for your business.
agenda_briefs.industries: Hotel & Accommodation, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Transportation & Logistics
agenda_briefs.source_body: 3.10 Stuart Ramsey - Need for Federal Regulations Regarding Aircraft Noise
agenda_briefs.article_title: Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submissions Agenda — July 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Council to consider aircraft noise complaint over North Burnaby
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a delegation from Willi Isaak and Ana Vuckovich regarding aircraft noise over North Burnaby. This is a proposed item on the council agenda — no decision has been made yet. The delegation will present concerns about noise from aircraft flying over the area. Council will listen to the presentation and may decide to take further action, such as referring the matter to staff for a report or contacting the relevant federal authority. Business owners in North Burnaby should be aware that this could lead to future noise regulations or operational changes affecting nearby businesses.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Monitor council's decision — if referred to staff, a report may recommend future noise restrictions that could affect your business operations.

key_dates.label: Council meeting — delegation on aircraft noise over North Burnaby

agenda_briefs.source_body: 3.11 Willi Isaak and Ana Vuckovich - Aircraft Noise Over North Burnaby
agenda_briefs.article_title: Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submissions Agenda — July 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Rezoning of 6470 and 6508 Silver Avenue in Burnaby
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed rezoning for two properties at 6470 and 6508 Silver Avenue. If approved, this rezoning would change the official land-use designation for these lots, potentially allowing for new development or a different type of building on the site. The decision has not been made yet, and the proposal is still under review.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Attend the public hearing or submit a written comment to Burnaby City Council before the vote to share your support or concerns.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 4.1 REZ #25-12 – 6470 and 6508 Silver Avenue
agenda_briefs.article_title: Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submissions Agenda — July 14, 2026
